steps.title: Set up the factorization and find the other factor
steps.content:
If $(x + 3b)$ is a factor, the quadratic must factor as $(x + 3b)(x + r)$ for some value $r$. Expanding:
$$(x + 3b)(x + r) = x^2 + (r + 3b)x + 3br$$
All choices have constant term $21b$. Setting $3br = 21b$ and dividing by the positive constant $b$:
$$3r = 21$$
$$r = 7$$

steps.title: Find which choice gives a positive integer b
steps.content:
With $r = 7$, the expression expands to $x^2 + (7 + 3b)x + 21b$. So the middle coefficient must equal $7 + 3b$. For each choice, solve for $b$:
  • A: $7 + 3b = 7$ → $b = 0$ ✗ (not positive)
  • B: $7 + 3b = 20$ → $b = \frac{13}{3}$ ✗ (not an integer)
  • C: $7 + 3b = 26$ → $b = \frac{19}{3}$ ✗ (not an integer)
  • D: $7 + 3b = 37$ → $b = 10$ ✓
D is correct.
